operatingQuestion: Can we answer model access, agent permissions, and governance controls without opening ten separate repos?
currentAnswer: The AI lane already has reusable scorecard, protocol, and bridge answers, but they still need one tighter standard packet.
headlineGap: Needs a single reusable questionnaire answer pack with canonical AI control language.
boardStory: AI trust answers are close to reusable productized diligence instead of bespoke consulting labor.
nextMove: Publish one AI governance answer pack tied to the scorecard, protocol suite, and watsonx bridge.

operatingQuestion: Can we answer access control, remediation ownership, and evidence retention without ad hoc drafting?
currentAnswer: The identity and app-protection lane has credible evidence, but the ownership model should be packaged more explicitly.
headlineGap: Needs clearer reusable response ownership across identity and endpoint questions.
boardStory: Identity diligence can be answered from one repeatable trust packet instead of scattered admin examples.
nextMove: Unify identity-risk, conditional-access, and app-protection answers into one enterprise answer pack.

operatingQuestion: Can we answer attribution, experimentation, and reporting-governance questions in a format procurement can trust?
currentAnswer: The reporting and MarTech proof is real, but the answers still read as a cluster of examples instead of one standard response set.
headlineGap: Answer library is fragmented across revenue, MarTech, and reporting surfaces.
boardStory: Revenue-system diligence becomes easier to sell when the answer library looks standardized instead of improvised.
nextMove: Turn reporting, attribution, and experimentation proof into one reusable response family.

operatingQuestion: Can we answer KYC, reserve risk, and payout-protection questions without legal rework every time?
currentAnswer: The payments, KYC, merchant, and treasury surfaces are now credible, but the questionnaire answers still need tighter legal-safe wording.
headlineGap: Needs standardized legal-safe answers and stronger evidence linkage for payments and KYC review.
boardStory: FinTech diligence turns into reusable trust capital when legal-safe response packs exist up front.
nextMove: Create one FinTech answer library spanning payments, merchant risk, KYC, and treasury.

buyer: Biotech quality and vendor qualification lead
questionnaireFamily: GxP quality and chain of custody
operatingQuestion: Can we answer CAPA, chain-of-custody, safety, and change-control questions without overclaiming compliance?
currentAnswer: The biotech proof is strong, but the answer language still needs one careful packet that keeps posture, evidence, and caveats together.
headlineGap: Needs a single qualification-ready answer pack for regulated biotech buyers.
boardStory: Regulated quality answers become much easier to trust when the narrative is standardized and evidence-linked.
nextMove: Package GxP, CAPA, specimen, and safety answers into one qualification-ready response set.

operatingQuestion: Can we answer RFP, disclosure, and trust-center questions in a format a public buyer can actually reuse?
currentAnswer: The ingredients exist, but there is no public-sector answer set yet that feels procurement-safe and explicitly reusable.
headlineGap: Blocked by missing public-sector answer pack and explicit response ownership.
boardStory: Public-sector expansion depends on packaging the answers, not inventing new capability.
nextMove: Build the first public-sector answer pack with disclosure, RFP, and trust-center modules.

operatingQuestion: Can we answer disclosure, safety, and patient-risk questions without sounding reckless or vague?
currentAnswer: The healthcare and disclosure estate already supports careful answers, but it still benefits from a standard response family.
headlineGap: Needs one healthcare trust pack that stays cautious and evidence-linked.
boardStory: Healthcare trust answers are strongest when standardized around restraint, disclosure, and evidence posture.
nextMove: Ship a clinical trust pack tied to care, diagnostics, and disclosure surfaces.
